I'll analyze the contract carefully and provide both the implementation and the complete specification.

## Implementation

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD)
}
```

## Lemma with `ensures` clauses

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  // 1. The kind is never changed
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind
  // 2. The numeric value is never changed
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value
  // 3. The canonical width is at least PAD (minimum 4 digits)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD
  // 4. The canonical width is at least the original width
  //    (we never shrink below the original — if original >= PAD, it stays unchanged)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= x.width
  // 5. If the input is already at or above PAD digits wide, width is unchanged
  ensures x.width >= PAD ==> Canonicalize(x).width == x.width
  // 6. If the input is below PAD digits wide, width is set to exactly PAD
  ensures x.width < PAD ==> Canonicalize(x).width == PAD
  // 7. The result is wellformed (canonical width can hold the value)
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))
  // 8. Canonicalizing an already-canonical result is a no-op (idempotence)
  ensures Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)
{ }
```

### Rationale for each clause

| # | Clause | Why it's needed |
|---|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | `kind` unchanged | Core invariant — identity of entity |
| 2 | `value` unchanged | Core invariant — which entity it refers to |
| 3 | `width >= PAD` | The "minimum 4 digits" requirement |
| 4 | `width >= x.width` | Never shrinks a wider id |
| 5 | `x.width >= PAD → width == x.width` | "Already at 4+ digits is returned unchanged" |
| 6 | `x.width < PAD → width == PAD` | Exactly pads to 4 when below |
| 7 | `Wellformed(result)` | Result is a valid id |
| 8 | Idempotence | "Canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op" |

Clauses 3 and 4 are technically implied by 5 and 6 together, but are stated explicitly to make the contract self-documenting and complete.
